# Agent Harness Landscape Report — March 2026

*Generated by AI agents using [OpenClaw](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw). Raw research data in [/research](/research).*

## What is Agent Harness Engineering?

OpenAI coined the term in February 2026: the discipline of designing environments, constraints, and feedback loops that make AI coding agents reliable at scale. Their team built 1M+ lines of production code with zero human-written lines using this approach.

The term caught fire. Within weeks, the ecosystem split into distinct layers — each solving a different part of the problem.

## The Stack

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                      Human Oversight                          │
│  Approve proposals · Review PRs · Set priorities · Override   │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                  Planning & Requirements                      │
│  Chorus · OpenSpec · Spec Kit · Kiro IDE                      │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                  Orchestration & Scheduling                   │
│  Vibe Kanban · Emdash · Symphony · Axon · Warp               │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                  Coding Agents (Execution)                     │
│  Claude Code · Codex · OpenCode · Gemini CLI · Amp · Kiro CLI │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                  Infrastructure                               │
│  MCP · agents.md · Git worktrees · CI/CD · Sandboxing         │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Competitive Landscape

### Orchestrators

| Tool | Approach | Agent Support | Parallel | Team/Cloud | Open Source |
|------|----------|--------------|----------|------------|-------------|
| **Vibe Kanban** | Kanban + worktrees | 10+ agents | ✅ | ✅ $30/user/mo | ✅ |
| **Emdash** | Desktop ADE | Provider-agnostic | ✅ | ❌ (local/SSH) | ✅ (YC W26) |
| **Warp** | Terminal IDE | Multiple | ✅ | ✅ | Partial |
| **VibeHQ** | CLI team orchestrator | Claude/Codex/Gemini | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ |
| **Beehive** | Multi-workspace | Claude/OpenCode | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ |

**Key observation:** All orchestrators focus on the execution layer — running multiple agents in parallel with worktree isolation. None manage where the tasks come from.

### Task Runners

| Tool | Source | Agent | Output |
|------|--------|-------|--------|
| **Symphony** (OpenAI) | Linear issues | Codex | PRs |
| **GitHub Copilot Agent** | GitHub Issues | Copilot | PRs |
| **Linear Coding Agent Harness** | Linear | Any | PRs |
| **Axon** | K8s CRDs / GitHub Issues | Any (container) | PRs + cost |

**Key observation:** Task runners automate the issue→code→PR pipeline but assume well-scoped issues already exist. The gap: who writes the issues?

### Planning & Requirements

| Tool | Focus | Workflow |
|------|-------|---------|
| **Chorus** | Full AI-DLC lifecycle | Idea → Elaboration → Proposal → Task DAG → Execute → Verify |
| **OpenSpec** | Spec generation | Natural language → structured spec |
| **Spec Kit** | Spec generation | GitHub-native spec workflow |
| **Kiro IDE** | Spec-driven development | Spec → code in IDE |

**Key observation:** This layer is the least crowded. Most teams still write specs manually or skip them entirely.

### Coding Agents

| Agent | Provider | Differentiator | npm Downloads (weekly) |
|-------|----------|---------------|----------------------|
| **Claude Code** | Anthropic | Agent Teams, hooks, skills | ~32M |
| **Codex** | OpenAI | Cloud sandbox, async | ~6M |
| **OpenCode** | SST (open source) | Plugin SDK, server mode | Growing fast |
| **Gemini CLI** | Google | Free tier, large context | New entrant |
| **Kiro CLI** | AWS | Spec-driven | New entrant |
| **Amp** | Sourcegraph | Code search integration | Niche |

**Key observation:** Claude Code dominates downloads 5:1 over Codex. The harness matters more than the model — OpenCode's plugin ecosystem is attracting developers despite using third-party models.

## Emerging Patterns

### 1. The Harness is the Product
OpenAI's blog made it clear: the agent is a commodity, the harness is the differentiator. Projects like "Everything Claude Code" (61K stars) and "Oh My OpenCode" (37K stars) prove demand for better harnesses around existing agents.

### 2. Convergence Toward Full Lifecycle
Vibe Kanban added Cloud team features. Symphony connects to Linear. GitHub launched Agentic Workflows. Everyone is expanding from their starting layer toward covering the full pipeline.

### 3. Agent-Agnostic is Winning
Tools that support multiple agents (Vibe Kanban: 10+, Emdash: any provider, Chorus: any MCP-compatible) are growing faster than single-agent tools. Developers don't want vendor lock-in.

### 4. The "Reversed Conversation" Gap
Most tools still follow the human→agent command pattern: human writes spec, agent executes. Few support the reverse: agent proposes, human approves. This is the frontier.

## Signals from This Week

- **Simon Willison** published "Anti-patterns: Agentic Engineering" — the community is maturing past hype into practical patterns
- **Cursor** launched JetBrains integration via ACP — the protocol wars are heating up (MCP vs ACP vs A2A)
- **OpenAI** reportedly building a GitHub alternative — vertical integration threat to the entire harness ecosystem
- **Latent Space** ran "Every Agent Needs a Box" with Aaron Levie — enterprise framing of the sandbox/harness problem
- **GitHub** launched Agentic Workflows — the largest platform entering the harness space directly

## What's Missing

1. **Cross-tool interoperability** — No standard way for a planning tool to hand off to an orchestrator to hand off to an agent
2. **Cost observability** — Only Axon reports execution cost. Most tools have no idea what a task costs to complete
3. **Failure recovery** — When an agent fails mid-task, most tools just... stop. Session recovery and retry patterns are primitive
4. **Multi-repo orchestration** — Most tools assume one repo. Real products span multiple repos
5. **Human-agent handoff protocols** — No standard for "agent got stuck, escalate to human with full context"

---
